ground_truth.final_diagnosis: Chronic Heart Failure with reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF)
ground_truth.diagnosis_explanation: The patient presents with classic symptoms of left-sided (dyspnea, orthopnea) and right-sided (ankle swelling) heart failure. Physical examination findings of elevated JVP, bilateral basal crepitations, and a third heart sound (S3) are highly suggestive. The diagnosis is confirmed by echocardiography showing reduced LVEF.
learning_objectives: Recognize symptoms and signs of chronic heart failure.
Understand the classification of heart failure based on ejection fraction.
Identify the four pillars of pharmacological treatment for HFrEF.
Interpret clinical findings in the context of heart failure pathophysiology.

history.opening_statement: I've been feeling really short of breath lately, especially when I'm moving around, and my ankles have been swelling up.
history.history_presenting_illness: Symptoms started gradually about three months ago. Initially, he noticed he couldn't walk to the shops without stopping. Now, he gets breathless just walking around the house. He has to sleep propped up on three pillows to breathe comfortably at night and occasionally wakes up gasping for air.
past_medical_history: History of myocardial infarction 5 years ago
family_history: Father died of a heart attack at age 60

general: Patient appears comfortable at rest but tachypneic on mild exertion
Bilateral pitting edema up to the mid-shin
abdominal: Liver edge palpable 2cm below costal margin

investigations.name: ECG
investigations.interpretation: Evidence of old anterior myocardial infarction

investigations.name: Chest X-ray
investigations.result: Cardiomegaly, prominent upper lobe venous diversion

investigations.name: NT-proBNP
investigations.interpretation: Significantly elevated, consistent with heart failure

key_history_questions: Review medication adherence and use of NSAIDs
Assess for symptoms of underlying IHD
reasoning.expected_final_diagnosis: Chronic Heart Failure with reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF)
management_plan: Initiate loop diuretics for symptom relief
Advise on salt restriction and daily weight monitoring

common_mistakes: Missing the use of NSAIDs as a precipitating factor
Failing to check for S3 gallop
Overlooking the need for SGLT2 inhibitors
high_yield_points: S3 is the most specific sign of chronic HF
Four pillars of HFrEF therapy reduce mortality
Echocardiography is the gold standard for diagnosis
lecture_references: Clinical presentation of chronic heart failure

behavior.communication_style: Polite, slightly anxious about his breathing, cooperative.
behavior.emotional_state: Concerned about his declining quality of life.
behavior.cooperativeness: High, willing to answer all questions.

never_disclose_unprompted: Exact EF percentage unless asked about test results
